chunk_summary: This section outlines the workflow for handling expressed breast milk in milk banking, including stop rules to ensure safety and quality, referencing guidelines on storage, preparation, and lactation management.
learning_objectives: Understand the workflow for handling expressed breast milk in milk banking.
Identify stop rules to ensure the safety and quality of expressed breast milk.
Learn proper storage and preparation techniques for expressed breast milk.
Familiarize with national guidelines for lactation management centers.
key_facts: Expressed breast milk must be stored at 4°C for up to 4 days or frozen at -20°C for longer storage (CDC guidelines).
Stop rules include discarding milk if improperly labeled, stored beyond recommended time, or contaminated.
Mismanagement of expressed milk can lead to safety risks, including bacterial contamination (Patient Safety Advisory).
Lactation management centers must adhere to national guidelines to ensure milk safety and quality (NHM guidelines).
Proper preparation of breast milk involves thawing in a refrigerator or warm water, avoiding microwaving (CDC guidelines).
reference_quote: Mismanagement of expressed breast milk can lead to safety risks, including bacterial contamination. (Patient Safety Advisory)
